overview: The MessageFormat Filter handles message formats commonly found in software applications, including **ICU Message Format** and **Java MessageFormat**. It is designed to be used as a **subfilter** — the message strings can live in various container formats such as JSON, YAML, XML, etc. The filter parses complex message syntax (plural, select, etc.) into translatable segments.

addPluralForms.help:
Adds missing plural forms to the source based on the **target locale's** CLDR plural rules. The source string is modified and re-filtered so that all required plural categories (e.g., `zero`, `one`, `two`, `few`, `many`, `other`) are presented to the translator.

This is useful when the source locale (e.g., English) has fewer plural categories than the target locale (e.g., Arabic with six categories).
notes: The source string is modified and then refiltered — this means the segment structure may change when this option is active.

normalize.help:
Moves leading and trailing text that appears outside complex message variants (`plural`, `select`, etc.) **inside each variant**. This forces each variant to be a complete phrase or sentence, which produces better translations.

For example, `Hello {count, plural, one {# item} other {# items}} found` becomes variants like `Hello # item found` and `Hello # items found`.
notes: **Warning:** This option can increase word and character counts for the source, since shared text is duplicated into each variant.

prettyPrint.help:
Formats the output with indentation and line breaks to enhance readability. Any whitespace added is **not significant** to the message format syntax.

When disabled (default), the output is a compact string, normally a single line.

limitations: `choice` syntax is **not supported** (e.g., `{count, choice, 0#is none|1#is one|1<is more than one}`). This syntax is deprecated and should be converted to `select` or `plural` before processing.
processingNotes: This filter is intended to be used as a **subfilter** — it processes message format strings extracted by a parent filter (JSON, YAML, XML, etc.).
Supports ICU Message Format and Java `MessageFormat` syntax, including `plural` and `select` message types.

examples.title: ICU plural message
examples.description: A typical ICU MessageFormat plural string that the filter processes. With `normalize` enabled, the surrounding text is pushed into each variant.
examples.input:
```
{count, plural, one {# item} other {# items}} found
```
